facts.label: THE 36-YEAR WAIT
facts.story: Canada's 1986 squad, coached by Welshman Tony Waiters, lost all three group games without scoring a single goal — a quiet full stop on a hard-fought qualifying run through CONCACAF. It would be 36 years before Canada returned to the World Cup, finally breaking through in 2022 behind a new generation led by Alphonso Davies and Jonathan David.

facts.label: THE FASTEST GOAL OF THE TOURNAMENT
facts.story: Two minutes into Canada's group match against Croatia in 2022, Alphonso Davies drilled home the earliest goal of the entire tournament — and the first World Cup goal in Canadian history. It came far too early to matter in the final score: Croatia answered with four unanswered goals to eliminate a Canadian side that had waited 36 years for this stage.

1986.headline: Three Games, No Goals

annotations.label: A QUIET DEBUT AGAINST FRANCE
annotations.story: In their first-ever World Cup match, Canada held reigning semifinalists France scoreless for most of the game in Léon before falling 1–0, a respectable start that proved to be the closest Canada would come to a result all tournament.

annotations.label: THREE LOSSES, ZERO GOALS
annotations.story: Defeats to Hungary and the Soviet Union followed, sending Canada out of the group stage without scoring a single goal across three matches — a wait for their first World Cup goal that would stretch on for 36 years.

annotations.label: A HIDDEN DIAMOND PATTERN
annotations.story: Canada's 1986 away jersey looked plain white from a distance, but up close carried a faint tonal diamond weave across the chest — a subtle Adidas signature of the mid-1980s — finished with a white V-neck collar and the brand's three white shoulder stripes. It remains one of the most obscure kits in World Cup history.

1986.homeColors: red shirt with thin pinstripes and white shorts (Adidas)

2022.headline: Ending a 36-Year Wait

annotations.label: BATSHUAYI BREAKS THE DEADLOCK
annotations.story: Michy Batshuayi's 44th-minute strike gave Belgium a 1–0 win in Canada's long-awaited World Cup return, a game Canada largely dominated but couldn't find an equalizer for despite a missed penalty by Alphonso Davies.

annotations.label: DAVIES BREAKS HIS OWN DROUGHT
annotations.story: Two minutes into the match against Croatia, Alphonso Davies scored Canada's first-ever World Cup goal — the fastest of the tournament. Croatia responded with four unanswered goals to seal a 4–1 win and end Canada's hopes early.

annotations.label: AGUERD'S OWN GOAL
annotations.story: Nayef Aguerd turned a Sam Adekugbe cross into his own net to put Canada ahead, but Morocco replied twice before halftime through Hakim Ziyech and Youssef En-Nesyri, beating Canada 2–1 to finish Group F on top.

2022.homeColors: red shirt with maple leaf-inspired pattern (Nike)

2026.headline: Home Soil, Historic Firsts
2026.homeColors: red shirt with tonal split maple-leaf graphic and black detailing, red shorts, red socks (Nike)
2026.awayColors: black shirt with distressed white cracked-ice maple-leaf graphic, University Red Swoosh and crest, black shorts, black socks (Nike)

matches.scorers: Larin 16', David 29', David 45'+3', Saliba 64', Manai OG 75', David 90'+2'

annotations.label: THE KEY MOMENT
annotations.story: Deep into second-half stoppage time of the Round of 32, Stephen Eustaquio killed a bouncing ball dead with his chest and drilled it into the bottom corner, beating South Africa 1–0 and sending co-host Canada into the knockout rounds for the first time. It came six days after Jonathan David's hat-trick delivered Canada's first-ever World Cup win, 6–0 over Qatar.

annotations.label: THE KIT
annotations.story: Nike dressed the co-hosts in red with a tonal, split maple-leaf graphic across the chest and matching shorts and socks for the home kit, while the away change flipped to black with a distressed, cracked-ice pattern nodding to Canadian winters, trimmed in University Red through the Swoosh and crest.

annotations.label: THE CONTEXT
annotations.story: David's stoppage-time finish against Qatar completed the first World Cup hat-trick by a North American player since Bert Patenaude's for the United States in 1930, and the 6–0 scoreline matched the largest victory margin ever posted by a host nation, alongside Italy's 1934 and Brazil's 1950 routs.
